error_signature: Supabase is set by default not to show more than 1000 records
title: Supabase limit on records for dropdown options
symptom: When trying to bind a large collection to a dropdown, it fails to show all options due to a default limit of 1000 records in Supabase.
applies_when: Using Supabase as a data source
Attempting to bind a dropdown to a large collection
not_this_if: The collection has fewer than 1000 records
root_cause: Supabase has a default setting that limits the number of records returned in a single API call to 1000. This can cause issues when trying to display a large dataset in a dropdown, as not all options will be available.
fix_steps: Change the settings to allow fetching more than 1000 items.
Fetch all elements to the collection and implement frontend pagination to limit the displayed items.
agent_instruction: Adjust the Supabase API settings to increase the record limit and implement frontend pagination.
